name: Bulgur Stuffed Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the core from the cabbage and place it in a steamer. Steam until all leaves are soft and separate easily.
2. In a large pan, sauté parsley, onions, celery, Italian seasoning, and garlic in an oil substitute until onions are soft.
3. Add the 2 cans of tomato sauce, 4 cups water, and bulgur to the pan. Cook over medium heat for about 1/2 hour, stirring occasionally, until the bulgur is tender.
4. Remove from heat. To stuff the cabbage leaves, place a spoonful of mixture in the center of each leaf.
5. Starting at one side, roll the leaf up and fold the ends under. Place the stuffed leaves in a deep baking pan.
6. Mix the 1/2 cup water with the contents of the 8 oz can of tomato sauce. Pour this mixture over the stuffed cabbage leaves to keep them moist during baking.
7. Bake at 375°F for about 30 minutes until the cabbage is hot.
